question_text: You have taken the wrong road and must turn around. How will you continue?

answers.text: I pay particular attention to oncoming traffic.
I drive up to the opening in the central reservation and hold back for any oncoming drivers.
I turn immediately to the left through the opening in the central reservation.
I keep going straight ahead at the intersection and find somewhere else to turn left.

explanation: As left-turns are forbidden at this intersection, you will have to keep going straight ahead and find somewhere else to turn left.

question_text: You have caught up with the car in front. It is moving slowly. How will you continue?

answers.text: I flash my headlights.
I increase my speed and overtake.
I will stay behind the driver in front.
I increase the distance between myself and the driver in front.

explanation: Due to the bend and the limited visibility, you must remain behind the driver in front for a while longer.

question_text: What does the policeman's hand signal mean?

answers.text: I must stop.
I may turn to the left at the intersection.
I may turn to the right at the intersection.
I may drive straight ahead at the intersection.

explanation: A policeman side-on to you means that you may turn to the left, turn to the right and drive straight ahead at the intersection.

question_text: You are driving at approximately 70 km/h. How will you continue?

answers.text: I reduce my speed.
I am ready to brake quickly.
I pull over as far as possible to the right of the traffic lane.
I am particularly mindful of possible obstacles on the other side of the hilltop.

explanation: You should pay particular attention to the course of the road, namely the hill that is obscuring your view. As you cannot see what is coming in terms of oncoming traffic from the other side of the hill, you should pull as far over to the right of the traffic lane as possible and reduce your speed, so that you can stop within the view length you have on the road.

question_text: You are looking for a parking space nearby. What do you do now?

answers.text: I drive up to the intersection and look to my side.
If the lane is clear, I keep going straight ahead at the intersection.
When the lane is clear, I must turn to the left at the intersection.
I hope that there is another parking space nearby.

explanation: You should pay particular attention to the "No entry".sign. Therefore, you must reduce your speed and turn left at the intersection when the lane is clear.

question_text: What should you pay particular attention to here?

answers.text: The maximum speed I can drive at in the centre lane is 50 km/h.
If I am driving at less than 50 km/h, I must use the lane furthest to the right.
If I am driving at more than 50 km/h, I must use the lane furthest to the right.
It is forbidden to use the lane on the right as it is reserved for lorries and buses.

explanation: Only vehicles that are not driving at the mandatory speed, in this case, at least 50 km/h, are allowed to use the crawler lane. When you cross over the wide, dotted edge line, you must follow the rules for changing lanes, whereby you must not constitute a risk or unnecessary inconvenience to other drivers.

question_text: What should you pay particular attention to here?

answers.text: The course of the road.
Railway crossing with barrier ahead.
Railway crossing without barrier ahead.
Dead-end road.

explanation: You should pay particular attention to the road signs warning of risky conditions on the road ahead, namely the railway crossing with a barrier. The distance marking below the road signs shows that there is 150-250 metres to the railway crossing.

question_text: You are driving at approximately 60 km/h and want to enter the motorway to the left. There are no drivers in the blind spot behind you. How will you continue?

answers.text: I increase my speed.
I activate my left indicator.
I merge in to the motorway in front of the lorry.
I brake and wait before changing lanes.

explanation: As there is a lorry, which may only drive at a maximum of 80 km/h, behind you on the motorway, you mustn't continue in your own lane but increase your speed and merge in to the motorway in front of the lorry.

question_text: What should you pay particular attention to here?

answers.text: Buses.
The course of the road.
The condition of the traffic lane.
Traffic lights.

explanation: The sign warns of traffic lights that may be difficult to see at a sufficiently long distance.

question_text: You have reduced your speed to 20 to 30 km/h due to the narrowing traffic lane. How will you continue?

answers.text: I continue at the same speed or a slightly higher speed.
I stop and wait for the oncoming traffic.
I pay particular attention to whether the oncoming driver is waiting for me.
Oncoming traffic should hold back for me.

explanation: The sign shows that the oncoming driver should wait for the give way sign "Wait for oncoming traffic". Therefore, if possible, you should increase your speed slightly in order to get past the narrowing of the road as quickly as possible. However, you should pay particular attention to whether the oncoming driver should now also wait, as he should, according to the sign.

question_text: You are driving at approximately 40 km/h and need to continue straight out the road. How will you continue?

answers.text: i pull over to the left side of the road.
I will continue driving at the same speed, i.e. approximately 40 km/h.
I reduce my speed.
I hold back for the oncoming traffic.

explanation: You cannot pass the lorry without going into the oncoming driver's lane. Therefore, you should brake and wait for the oncoming driver.

question_text: You are about to catch up the driver in front who is driving at approximately 60 km/h. There is no traffic behind you. How will you continue?

answers.text: I flash my full-beam headlights.
I overtake the car.
I reduce my speed.
I wait a bit longer before overtaking the driver in front.

explanation: Since there is oncoming traffic, you cannot overtake. Therefore, you must reduce your speed and wait before overtaking the driver in front.

question_text: You are driving at approximately 50 km/h, and there is no traffic behind you. The oncoming truck is about to turn right into the side road. How will you continue?

answers.text: I pay particular attention to the oncoming lorry.
I pull further over to the right of the traffic lane.
I reduce my speed.
I hold back for the oncoming traffic.

explanation: The lorry driver wants to turn to the right, therefore you must pay particular attention to it due to its need for plenty or space, which means that there is a risk it will come into the centre of the road. Therefore, you should pull further over to the right of the traffic lane and move your foot on to the brake.

question_text: What should you pay particular attention to here?

answers.text: I may turn to the right at the intersection.
It is forbidden to turn to the right at the intersection.
I may turn to the left at the intersection.
It is forbidden to turn to the left at the intersection.

explanation: As right-turns are forbidden at the intersection, you will have to turn to the left.

question_text: You are driving at approximately 50 km/h and the driver behind you has caught up with you. What do you do?

answers.text: I increase my speed.
I pull in to the right.
I reduce my speed.
I stop completely.

explanation: You must not increase your speed as you are driving in a heavily built-up area. The lorry driver is driving dangerously close to you and apparently wants to get past you. Due to the lorry driver's limited ability to brake, it would probably be best that you pull as far over to the right of the traffic lane as possible and reduce your speed slightly to let the lorry past.

question_text: You are about to drive off from a parking space. What should you pay particular attention to?

answers.text: I should wait for the tractor coming from the left.
The tractor from the left should wait for me.
I should wait for any traffic coming from the right.
I should wait for any traffic coming from the left.

explanation: You should be particularly mindful of the fact that when you are leaving a parking space, you have an unconditional duty to give way and must wait for traffic from both the left and the right. You may only drive off when you are not posing a risk or causing inconvenience.

question_text: You are driving at approximately 40 km/h and need to continue straight out the road. What do you do here?

answers.text: I will continue driving at the same speed, i.e. approximately 40 km/h.
I pull further over to the right of the traffic lane.
I stop in front of the pedestrian crossing.

explanation: You should pay particular attention to the pedestrian, who is on their way over the pedestrian crossing. So, move your foot on to the brake and wait for the pedestrian. You should not pull further to the right of the traffic lane.

question_text: You want to turn to the left at this intersection. What should you pay particular attention to here?

answers.text: I wait for cars and motorcycles coming from the right.
I wait for cars and motorcycles coming from the left.
I wait for cyclists coming from the right.
I wait for cyclists coming from the left

explanation: The road sign indicates that you will cross a two-lane cycle path. Therefore, you should pay particular attention to cyclists coming from both the left and the right.

question_text: You are about to drive on to a motorway. The motorist in front is hesitating about merging in. There is no traffic behind you in the nearest lane on the actual motorway. What do you do?

answers.text: I apply my brakes.
I will stay behind the drivers in front in the meantime.
I drive into the motorway over the dotted lines immediately.
I continue on the access lane for a while longer.

explanation: You have to stay behind the driver in front and let him drive on to the motorway first. Even though the road behind is clear, you mustn't drive into the motorway straight away but continue in the access lane until the dotted lines end.

question_text: The driver has left the grey car here. Which rules for stopping and parking apply here?

answers.text: May you leave the car here for up to 3 minutes?
May you park the car here?
It is forbidden to stop in the viaduct?

explanation: It is forbidden to stop under a bridge, in a viaduct or in a tunnel.

question_text: You have caught up with the lorry, which is moving slowly, and you would really like to get on quickly. There is no traffic behind you. How will you continue?

answers.text: I give a signal for overtaking with my horn.
I flash my headlights.
I overtake.
I reduce my speed and stay behind the lorry.

explanation: Give the signal with your lights because it is in the regulations that if you can see that the driver in front is not aware that you want to overtake, then give the signal with your lights or horn. The horn must not be used unless the danger is imminent.

question_text: You need to go straight ahead at the intersection. How will you continue?

answers.text: I pay particular attention to the pedestrian.
I look to see what is happening on both sides before I drive into the intersection.
There is an unconditional duty to give way at the intersection.
I am particularly aware that I am driving into an area with speed suppression measures.

explanation: You don't need to pay particular attention to the pedestrian who is on their way across the traffic lane on the opposite side of the intersection, but pay particular attention to any traffic crossing at the intersection. Here, there are Give Way lines, which means there is an unconditional duty to give way. There is no road sign indicating that you are driving into an area with speed suppression measures, therefore you are not driving in an area with speed suppression measures.

question_text: You are driving at approximately 20 to 30 km/h. How will you continue?

answers.text: I will continue driving at the same speed, 20 to 30 km/h.
I am particularly mindful of the cyclist in front.
I brake hard immediately.
I pull over further to the left of the traffic lane.

explanation: Here, it is the pedestrian that presents the nearest hazard. Therefore, you should not pay particular attention to the cyclist. The pedestrian is on his way into your traffic lane. Therefore, you should brake hard and not move any further over to the left.

question_text: You have gotten out of the red car.

answers.text: May you leave the car here for 2-3 minutes?
May you park the car here?
Is it forbidden to stop the car here?

explanation: It is forbidden to stop, and therefore park, closer than 10 metres from an intersection.

question_text: You have gotten out of the red car.

answers.text: May you leave the car here for 2-3 minutes?
May you park the car here?
Is stopping and parking the car forbidden here?

explanation: It is forbidden to stop and park in a spot marked for hire cars.
